skill.name: fractal-docs-generator
skill.description: Generates and maintains directory-level CLAUDE.md documentation when project folders or file structures change.
skill.summary: Create concise folder-level CLAUDE.md maps for codebases.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 12, 19, and 34. These lines are Markdown code fences, not Ruby or shell execution. The weak cryptography hit at line 3 is a false positive from CLAUDE.md text.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported backtick execution locations are Markdown fences used to show CLAUDE.md examples. They do not invoke Ruby, shell commands, or dynamic execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain Markdown code fence markers, not shell or Ruby execution syntax. The file is documentation-only and contains no executable script body.

low_findings.title: False Positive: CLAUDE.md Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert maps to descriptive text naming CLAUDE.md. No MD5, SHA1, encryption API, hashing function, or security-sensitive code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is front matter describing directory-level CLAUDE.md generation. The alert appears to match the '.md' filename extension rather than a cryptographic operation.

content.value_statement: Codebases become harder to navigate when folder responsibilities are undocumented. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to create concise CLAUDE.md files that explain directory roles and key files.
actual_capabilities: Creates a directory-level CLAUDE.md template with a short folder purpose.
Documents important files with role and responsibility columns.
Classifies files as entry, core, helper, type, configuration, or test files.
Provides a concrete auth directory example for consistent formatting.
Defines when documentation should be refreshed after directory changes.
limitations: Does not inspect files automatically without the host AI reading the codebase.
Does not generate file-level headers or per-file documentation.
Does not run commands, modify directories, or validate documentation freshness.
Uses a compact fixed format that may need adaptation for non-code folders.

use_cases.title: Document a New Feature Folder
use_cases.description: Create a CLAUDE.md file after adding a feature directory so future agents understand the folder purpose.

use_cases.title: Map Legacy Code Areas
use_cases.description: Summarize important files in an existing directory before refactoring or onboarding new contributors.

use_cases.title: Keep Agent Context Local
use_cases.description: Maintain small directory notes that help AI coding tools reason about nearby files during edits.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Folder Map
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a CLAUDE.md file for this directory using the fractal-docs-generator format. Include the folder purpose and a table of important files.

prompt_templates.title: Update After File Changes
prompt_templates.scenario: Refresh documentation after adding, moving, or renaming files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this directory and update its CLAUDE.md file. Reflect new, moved, or renamed files and keep each responsibility concise.

prompt_templates.title: Classify File Roles
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a folder map with role labels.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the files in this directory and classify each documented file as entry, core, helper, type, configuration, or test. Update CLAUDE.md with clear responsibilities.

prompt_templates.title: Normalize Nested Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: For these related directories, create or update directory-level CLAUDE.md files. Keep each file focused on local responsibilities and avoid duplicating file-level header documentation.

output_examples.input: A new auth directory contains index.ts, auth.service.ts, and jwt.helper.ts.
output_examples.output: A CLAUDE.md summary that names auth as authentication and authorization, then lists each file with entry, core, and helper roles.

output_examples.input: A service folder gained config.ts and service.test.ts after a refactor.
output_examples.output: An updated folder document that adds configuration and test rows while preserving the directory purpose.

output_examples.input: A developer wants AI agents to understand a complex utilities folder.
output_examples.output: A concise directory map that separates public entry files from helpers and describes each responsibility in one phrase.

best_practices: Update the directory CLAUDE.md whenever files are created, moved, renamed, or removed.
Write responsibilities as short functional descriptions, not implementation histories.
Keep directory-level notes separate from detailed file-level documentation.
anti_patterns: Do not list every generated artifact when it does not help navigation.
Do not copy full source code or long explanations into CLAUDE.md.
Do not let folder documentation drift after structural changes.

faq.question: What does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates a concise CLAUDE.md file for a directory, including purpose, file roles, and responsibilities.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after creating, moving, renaming, or changing files in a directory.

faq.question: Does it execute commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance only and does not define scripts or command execution.

faq.question: Can it replace full project documentation?
faq.answer: No. It is best for local folder context and should complement broader architecture documents.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks it as compatible with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What format does it recommend?
faq.answer: It recommends a short heading and a table with file, role, and responsibility columns.
